summary: A brief Q&A with Lynda Resnick detailing her daily habits, boundaries with technology, and sources of energy. She emphasizes disconnecting from emails after 7 P.M., keeping her phone number private, and prioritizing family during free moments.

principles.statement: Establish strict boundaries with digital communication.
principles.rationale: To maintain personal time and prevent technology from encroaching on rest, one must set hard cut-offs for emails and phone availability.
principles.source_quote: As they come in but not after 7:00 pm.

principles.rationale: Business success should be tied to giving back to the communities where employees live and work through health, wellness, and educational initiatives.

heuristics: Never give out your personal phone number.
Use unexpected 15-minute breaks to call family members.

signature_quotes.text: No and I never give out my number.
signature_quotes.context: When asked if her phone sleeps with her.

signature_quotes.text: As they come in but not after 7:00 pm.
signature_quotes.context: When asked how she deals with email.

anti_patterns.description: Sleeping with your phone or giving out your personal number.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It removes personal boundaries and invites constant interruption.
